title: signInFormHook.ts — validateEmailOrUsername rejects disposable email subdomains
commit_message: fix: block common disposable domains in sign-in validation

why_auth_hook.explanation: signInFormHook.ts is listed in sources[] only for the four 'validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX' tests in auth-storage.cross.test.js. Those are CRITICAL. Every other describe in the same file is HIGH (sibling inclusion after a CRITICAL hit) — isUserLoggedIn, updateNewTokenDetails, generateCardNumber, capitalizeFirstLetter — mirroring the blast pattern of scenario 9’s cross-dependent slice.
why_auth_hook.key_point: 4 CRITICAL + 9 HIGH = 13 selected; 63 SKIP — no standalone suite lists signInFormHook.ts without also listing constants.ts for a different describe.

CRITICAL.reason: symbols[] contains validateEmailOrUsername; sources include signInFormHook.ts

tests.test: validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes input containing @ to email validation and accepts a valid address
tests.result: ⚠️  MAY FAIL — disposable-domain list may reject previously valid addresses

tests.test: validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > routes plain text input to username validation and accepts a clean username
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the email format is invalid via EMAIL_REGEX
validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX > returns an error message when the username contains special characters

HIGH.reason: Same cross-dependent file — sibling describe blocks

tests.test: isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns false when no token is stored
isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns true after a token has been stored under storageKeys.token
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the refresh token under the REFRESHTOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the login time under the LOGGEDINTIME key
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber contains only digits and spaces
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes the first letter of each word in a multi-word string
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes a single word correctly without whitespace branch

SKIP.reason: signInFormHook.ts not in sources[] for standalone, payment, or feature suites

coverage_gaps.detail: Add tests for disposable domains (e.g. mailinator.com) returning a validation error.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "validateEmailOrUsername uses EMAIL_REGEX|isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token|updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX|capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace" --verbose
